title: The Marketing Mix (4Ps)
summary: Product, Price, Place, and Promotion — the core marketing mix framework with extended 7Ps.
content_html:
The Marketing Mix

The marketing mix is the set of controllable tactical tools a company uses to produce the response it wants in the target market. The traditional framework is the 4Ps.

1. Product

What the company sells — it's not just the physical item but the total value bundle:

  • Core product: The fundamental benefit/need it satisfies.
  • Actual product: Physical features, quality level, brand name, packaging, design.
  • Augmented product: Additional services — warranty, after-sale support, delivery.

Product decisions: product range (mix), product lifecycle (introduction → growth → maturity → decline), branding, packaging.

2. Price

The amount customers pay. Pricing strategies:

  • Cost-plus: Cost + markup = price.
  • Competitive pricing: Set relative to competitors.
  • Value-based pricing: Based on perceived customer value.
  • Penetration pricing: Low initial price to gain market share.
  • Skimming: High initial price, then lower over time.
  • Psychological pricing: $9.99 instead of $10.

3. Place (Distribution)

How products reach customers — distribution channels:

  • Direct channel: Producer → Customer (own website, factory store).
  • Indirect channel: Producer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Customer.
  • Intensive distribution: Maximum outlets (e.g., Coca-Cola).
  • Selective distribution: Some outlets (e.g., consumer electronics).
  • Exclusive distribution: Limited outlets (e.g., luxury brands).

4. Promotion

How the company communicates and persuades the target market:

  • Advertising: Paid media (TV, social, print).
  • Public Relations (PR): Managing brand reputation.
  • Sales Promotion: Short-term incentives (discounts, coupons, contests).
  • Personal Selling: One-on-one sales.
  • Direct Marketing: Email, direct mail, telemarketing.
  • Digital/Social Media Marketing: Online channels.

Extended 7Ps (Services Marketing)

For services, add: People (staff quality), Process (service delivery process), Physical Evidence (tangible cues like store design).

exercises.title: 4Ps of marketing
exercises.prompt: The 4Ps of the marketing mix are:

exercises.title: Penetration pricing
options: Setting a low initial price to gain market share
Pricing based on cost plus markup
exercises.answer: Setting a low initial price to gain market share

exercises.title: Intensive distribution
options: Maximize the number of sales outlets
exercises.answer: Maximize the number of sales outlets

exercises.title: Price skimming
options: Starting with a high price then lowering it over time
exercises.answer: Starting with a high price then lowering it over time

exercises.title: PR in promotion
options: Managing brand reputation and media relationships
exercises.answer: Managing brand reputation and media relationships

exercises.title: Place P definition
exercises.prompt: The 'Place' P in marketing refers to:
options: Distribution channels through which products reach customers
The physical location of the company
exercises.answer: Distribution channels through which products reach customers

exercises.title: Value-based pricing
exercises.prompt: Value-based pricing sets price based on:

exercises.title: 7Ps additional Ps
exercises.prompt: The extended 7Ps adds three elements over the 4Ps:

exercises.title: Sales promotion
options: Short-term incentives like discounts or coupons
exercises.answer: Short-term incentives like discounts or coupons
